page: Amazon Web Services a1.4xlarge is a virtualized server instance powered by the first-generation AWS Graviton processor. Built on the ARMv8 architecture using Cortex-A72 cores, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local NVMe storage, does not include a GPU, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile characterized by moderate multi-core capabilities and weaker single-core execution. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test scores 23858.27, placing it in the average tier, while the single-core CPU test scores 1491.48. Memory bandwidth shows a stark contrast between cached and uncached operations; small-block cached reads reach an average of 566.18 GB/sec, whereas large-block uncached writes drop to a poor 10.01 GB/sec. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression register in the bottom 10% of benchmarks. However, the instance maintains average performance in database operations, scoring 4180.93 in PassMark, and handles Redis SET operations at 8.36 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the a1.4xlarge offers an entry-level ARM-based hardware profile that trades raw single-threaded performance for dedicated physical core density. Because it lacks hyperthreading, each of the 16 vCPUs maps to a dedicated physical core, reducing resource contention. This architecture is suitable for scale-out workloads that can leverage parallel execution, such as static web serving, basic database operations, and software compilation, rather than memory-intensive or high-throughput serialization tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services a1.4xlarge is an ARM64-based instance powered by first-generation AWS Graviton Cortex-A72 processors. It features 16 dedicated physical cores, 16 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps, running on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks reveal average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 23858.27 and Redis SET throughput of 8.36 million operations per second, contrasted by weak single-core and uncached memory write performance. This instance provides a cost-efficient entry point into ARM-based computing, trading single-threaded speed for dedicated core density. It is best suited for parallelized workloads, software compilation, and basic database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services a1.4xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network speed, offering cost-efficient multi-core performance for parallelized workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services a1.4xlarge is an ARM64 server with 16 dedicated Graviton cores and 32 GB RAM, optimized for parallel scale-out workloads.
tagline: A dedicated ARM64 instance offering sixteen physical cores for parallelized workloads and cost-efficient scale-out applications.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv8 Cortex-A72 physical cores.
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers average multi-core performance with 8.36 million Redis SET operations per second.
Exhibits lower single-threaded performance and uncached memory write speeds.
Suitable for software compilation, static web serving, and parallelized scale-out workloads.
